assertions.title: Sales dispatch confirm is the only public write owner

steps.action: Probe the canonical sales route with sales vs factory actors using empty JSON bodies
steps.expected: The surviving canonical route is reachable for sales-owned dispatch validation while non-owner factory users are denied.
steps.observed: POST /api/v1/sales/dispatch/confirm returned 400 VAL_001 for validation.sales@example.com (`Dispatch confirmation requires transporterName or driverName`), proving the route is live past auth; the same request returned 403 AUTH_004 for validation.factory@example.com with denial copy telling the operator to use the factory dispatch workspace for reads and sales for final posting.

steps.action: Probe the retired alias and inspect OpenAPI path ownership
steps.expected: No second public dispatch-confirm write route remains.
steps.observed: POST /api/v1/dispatch/confirm returned 404 Not Found, while openapi.json exposes /api/v1/sales/dispatch/confirm and does not list /api/v1/dispatch/confirm.

assertions.title: Factory dispatch namespace is operational read-only

steps.action: Inspect the dispatch namespace methods in openapi.json
steps.expected: The surviving /api/v1/dispatch/** surface exposes GET routes only for pending, preview, slip, and order lookup.
steps.observed: The OpenAPI summary lists only GET for /api/v1/dispatch/pending, /api/v1/dispatch/preview/{slipId}, /api/v1/dispatch/slip/{slipId}, and /api/v1/dispatch/order/{orderId}; no write verb exists under the dispatch namespace.

steps.action: Send negative write probes to the dispatch namespace
steps.observed: POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE against /api/v1/dispatch/pending all returned 405 Method Not Allowed; the retired POST /api/v1/dispatch/confirm alias returned 404.

assertions.title: Surviving dispatch reads remain operational and redacted

steps.action: Probe the surviving pending-slip read on the seeded runtime
steps.expected: The factory/operator read surface remains reachable after the write hard-cut.
steps.observed: GET /api/v1/dispatch/pending returned 200 for both validation.factory@example.com and validation.sales@example.com; the seeded runtime currently has an empty pending list, but the read surface is operational.

steps.action: Corroborate preview/slip/order redaction with targeted dispatch tests
steps.expected: Factory-facing dispatch reads stay redacted for commercial fields while sales views retain financial fields.
steps.observed: DispatchControllerTest passed redaction-focused cases `getPendingSlips_filtersDispatchedAndRedactsFactoryView`, `factoryViews_areRedactedForPreviewAndSlipDetails`, `factorySlipView_redactsLineUnitCost`, `salesViewsRetainFinancialFieldsWithoutFactoryRedaction`, and `getPackagingSlipByOrder_salesViewsRetainFinancialFields`; DispatchOperationalBoundaryIT also passed `factoryDispatchFlow_redactsCommercialFields_persistsLogistics_andReplaysWithoutDuplicateMovements`.

frictions.description: The seeded validation runtime did not start with any pending dispatch slips, so live preview/slip/order probes could not be completed from the default seed.
frictions.resolution: Used the live 200 pending-surface probe plus deterministic DispatchControllerTest and DispatchOperationalBoundaryIT redaction coverage to prove the surviving read contract without fabricating runtime fixtures.
